strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity)
properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal (often collective)
a mountain
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. (by implication) to be safe; causatively, to free or succor

properly, a verdict (favorable or unfavorable) pronounced judicially, especially a sentence or formal decree (human or (participant's) divine law, individual or collective), including the act, the pla
rightness (abstractly), subjectively (rectitude), objectively (justice), morally (virtue) or figuratively (prosperity)
abundant (in quantity, size, age, number, rank, quality)
an abyss (as a surging mass of water), especially the deep (the main sea or the subterranean watersupply)

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.
צְדָקָה — "righteousness" (moral quality) vs. "justice" (social-legal action). OT prophets use this for God's vindicating action for the oppressed; "righteousness" alone misses the social dimension.

tokens.range: ruddy i.e. a human being (an individual or the species, mankind, etc.)

gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe

to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in
valuable (objectively or subjectively)

shade, whether literal or figurative

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.
